$comment: c76 (D-FF3-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-STACK-OVERFLOW-OFFSET, folded into c76; 2026-07-01): on x86-64 SysV a callee with BOTH GPR-class params overflowing past 6 GPR arg regs AND FP-class params overflowing past 8 FP arg regs must read the overflowed GPR stack arg and the overflowed FP stack arg from DISTINCT monotonic incoming-stack offsets. Pre-fix, the per-class overflow counters each restart the cursor at 0 so the FP overflow reads the INT overflow slot (garbage double). WITNESS: int probe(int i1..i7, double d0..d8) -> isum(28) + (int)dsum(45) = 73. RED-ON-DISABLE (WITNESSED on build-rel): revert the D-FF3 offset fix -> SysV returns 64 not 73; AAPCS64/Win64 slot-align so they are 73 regardless. RE-ARMED with the `release` optimizedPipelines arm 2026-07-07: the FP-constant-un-loaded-xmm bug that formerly forced this to omit the release arm (D-OPT-RELEASE-FP-CONSTANT-UNLOADED-XMM) shared ONE root with the mixed-class inline arg-drop and was fixed by D-OPT-RELEASE-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-REG-ARG-DROP (the MIR Arg now carries a flat call-operand position so the inliner maps the FP actuals correctly); the release build now returns 73 on all 3 run legs. This is BOTH the D-FF3 stack-offset witness AND a release-composition witness for the arg-position fix.
